anchor.snippet: All finite–dimensional normed spaces are separable Banach spaces

anchor.snippet: Every separable infinite–dimensional Hilbert space is linearly isometrically isomorphic

annotations.note: Any Hilbert space is isometrically isomorphic to ℓ²(ι,𝕜) via a Hilbert basis; the separable case has countable ι.

annotations.note: The Anderson–Kadec homeomorphism theorem is not in Mathlib.

annotations.note: Banach manifolds modeled on metric Banach spaces are not formalized.

annotations.label: Topological vector space

annotations.note: Mathlib has no single TVS class; the notion is the combination Module + ContinuousSMul + IsTopologicalAddGroup.

annotations.label: Mazur–Ulam theorem

anchor.snippet: the Mazur–Ulam theorem states that

annotations.note: An isometric bijection between real normed (affine) spaces is affine, via the Mazur-Ulam conversions (toRealLinearIsometryEquiv / toRealAffineIsometryEquiv).

annotations.label: Finite-dimensional homeomorphism classification

anchor.snippet: Finite dimensional Banach spaces are homeomorphic as topological spaces, if and only if they have the same dimension

annotations.note: Equal finite dimension gives a linear homeomorphism, but the converse (different dimensions are not homeomorphic) is not packaged.

annotations.label: Separable Banach spaces are homeomorphic

anchor.snippet: any two infinite-dimensional separable Banach spaces are homeomorphic as topological spaces

annotations.note: The Kadec/Anderson homeomorphism classification is not formalized.

annotations.note: Toruńczyk's density-character classification is not formalized.
